name: Thai Basil Chicken (Easy Gai Pad Krapow)
description: This Thai basil chicken recipe takes just 3 minutes to prepare and 7 minutes to cook. Served along with steamed rice, it's restaurant food, fast.
recipeIngredient: 3  Thai bird chilies or holland chilies
1/3 cup low sodium chicken broth ((or water))
1  bunch holy or Thai basil leaves
recipeInstructions: In a wok over high heat, add the oil, chilies, shallots and garlic, and fry for 1-2 minutes. Add the ground chicken and stir-fry for 2 minutes, breaking up the chicken into small bits.
Add the sugar, soy sauce, and fish sauce. Stir-fry for another minute and deglaze the pan with the broth. Because your pan is over high heat, the liquid should cook off very quickly.
Add the basil, and stir-fry until wilted. Serve over rice.
author: Sarah

ai.cliffNotes: Fry aromatics and chilies first to infuse the oil, then cook ground chicken quickly over high heat while building flavor with umami-rich sauces and finishing with fresh basil for brightness.
keyTips: Break up the ground chicken into small pieces as it cooks so it seasons evenly and develops better texture
Keep heat high throughout so the broth deglazes and evaporates quickly, concentrating flavors rather than stewing the dish
Add basil at the very end and stir only until just wilted to preserve its fresh, aromatic quality
Slice the garlic and chilies rather than mincing so they stay partially intact and won't burn during the initial frying
